description: D-23: Schema-qualified "public.users" resolves to "users"
rationale: Grounding strips schema qualifiers; "public.users" is not a recognized table name in init.sql

description: D-23: Unqualified "users" table exists (control)
rationale: Control: unqualified table name matches init.sql directly

description: D-23: Schema-qualified "public.sessions" column check
rationale: Schema-qualified table name not recognized by grounding gate

description: D-24: Edit adds GENERATED column, predicate checks it exists
rationale: Migration adds a GENERATED ALWAYS column; predicate should find it in modified init.sql

description: D-24: Predicate asserts computed column that was never added
rationale: No GENERATED column exists — pure fabrication

description: D-25: Edit adds COLLATE to username, predicate checks column type

edits.replace: username VARCHAR(50) COLLATE "en_US.utf8" NOT NULL UNIQUE

rationale: COLLATE is a modifier, not a type change — column_type should still match VARCHAR(50)

description: D-25: Predicate expects TEXT after collation change (wrong type)

edits.replace: username VARCHAR(50) COLLATE "C" NOT NULL UNIQUE

rationale: Column is still VARCHAR(50) despite collation annotation — TEXT assertion is wrong

description: D-26: Edit adds partial index, predicate checks underlying column

edits.replace:
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_expires ON sessions(expires_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_active_users ON users(username) WHERE is_active = true;

rationale: Partial index on existing column — column_exists check passes independently

description: D-26: Predicate references expression index column that does not exist

edits.replace:
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_expires ON sessions(expires_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_lower_email ON users(LOWER(email));

rationale: Expression index LOWER(email) does not create a column called lower_email

description: D-27: Edit adds table with composite PK, predicate checks both columns
rationale: Composite PK table added — both columns exist in the new table

description: D-27: Predicate checks composite PK column on wrong table
rationale: role_name does not exist on users table — confusing composite key table with parent

description: D-28: Column exists regardless of ordinal position (id is first)
rationale: column_exists is order-independent — id is first but check does not depend on position

description: D-28: Column type correct regardless of position (created_at is last)
rationale: Type check is position-agnostic — created_at is last column but type matches

description: D-28: Edit reorders columns, predicate still finds column

edits.search:
id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
edits.replace:
username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,

rationale: Column order changed but type is still correct — assertion is order-independent

description: D-29: Edit adds trigger function + trigger, predicate checks updated_at column
rationale: Trigger function added but updated_at column already exists — check passes

description: D-29: Predicate checks for trigger-managed column that does not exist
rationale: posts table has no updated_at column — trigger would not create it

description: D-30: Edit adds VIEW, predicate checks view as table_exists

edits.replace:
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_token ON sessions(token);
CREATE VIEW active_users AS SELECT id, username FROM users WHERE is_active = true;

rationale: VIEW definition found by grounding in init.sql — table_exists matches CREATE VIEW

description: D-30: Predicate checks MATERIALIZED VIEW column as regular column

edits.replace:
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_token ON sessions(token);
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW post_stats AS SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) as post_count FROM posts GROUP BY user_id;

rationale: MATERIALIZED VIEW columns appear in the CREATE statement — grounding can parse them

description: D-30: Predicate checks fabricated view name
rationale: No view or table named user_summary exists in schema

description: D-31: Edit makes FK deferrable, predicate checks FK column still exists

edits.search:
user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    title VARCHAR(200)
edits.replace:
user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED,
    title VARCHAR(200)

rationale: DEFERRABLE is a constraint modifier, not a type change — column is still INTEGER

description: D-31: Predicate expects FK column type includes DEFERRABLE (wrong)

edits.search:
user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    title VARCHAR(200)
edits.replace:
user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) DEFERRABLE,
    title VARCHAR(200)

rationale: DEFERRABLE is not part of the column type — type is still INTEGER

description: D-32: Edit adds column via ALTER TABLE appended to init.sql, predicate checks new column
rationale: ALTER TABLE appended to init.sql — grounding should pick up the new column

description: D-32: Predicate checks column that was dropped in same migration
rationale: Grounding parses CREATE TABLE (is_active exists) but may not process ALTER TABLE DROP — stale cache scenario

description: D-32: Predicate checks column from ALTER TABLE when CREATE TABLE has no such column
rationale: ALTER TABLE adds slug — grounding should detect it if it parses ALTER statements

description: D-13: Predicate asserts row_count on existing table (unsupported assertion type)
rationale: row_count is not a supported assertion type (only table_exists, column_exists, column_type)

description: D-13: Predicate uses table_exists as proxy for non-empty table (control)
rationale: Control: table_exists is the supported way to verify schema presence

description: D-14: Predicate asserts specific row value (unsupported assertion)
rationale: row_value is not a supported assertion — only schema-level checks supported

description: D-14: Predicate checks column type of JSONB value column (control)
rationale: Control: column_type JSONB is the valid structural assertion

description: D-15: Predicate checks SERIAL column type (auto-increment)
rationale: users.id is declared as SERIAL — type assertion matches

description: D-15: Predicate expects INTEGER for SERIAL column (partial match)
rationale: SERIAL is declared type in init.sql — grounding matches literal type string, not underlying INTEGER

description: D-15: Predicate expects BIGSERIAL for SERIAL column (wrong precision)
rationale: Column is SERIAL not BIGSERIAL — precision mismatch

description: D-17: Edit adds isolation-sensitive column, predicate checks it

edits.replace:
view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    lock_version INTEGER DEFAULT 0,

rationale: Optimistic locking column added — structural check passes regardless of isolation level

description: D-17: Predicate checks for transaction-specific column not in schema
rationale: xmin is a PostgreSQL system column not declared in init.sql — grounding rejects

description: D-33: Nullable column (posts.body) exists and is TEXT
rationale: posts.body is TEXT and nullable — type assertion is about declared type, not null state

description: D-33: Predicate expects NOT NULL on nullable column (wrong)
rationale: posts.body is TEXT without NOT NULL — "TEXT NOT NULL" does not match declared type

description: D-33: NOT NULL column correctly typed
rationale: posts.title is VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL — type check ignores constraint modifiers

description: D-34: Edit adds NUMERIC column, predicate checks type

edits.replace:
view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    rating NUMERIC(3,2) DEFAULT 0.00,

rationale: NUMERIC(3,2) added — exact precision type match

description: D-34: Predicate expects FLOAT for NUMERIC column (wrong type)

edits.replace:
view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    rating NUMERIC(3,2) DEFAULT 0.00,

rationale: Column is NUMERIC(3,2) not FLOAT — precision type mismatch

description: D-34: DOUBLE PRECISION vs REAL type confusion

edits.replace:
view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    score DOUBLE PRECISION DEFAULT 0.0,

rationale: DOUBLE PRECISION is not REAL — different precision levels

description: D-35: Existing TIMESTAMP column (naive) type check
rationale: users.created_at is declared as TIMESTAMP (without time zone)

description: D-35: Predicate expects TIMESTAMPTZ for TIMESTAMP column (wrong)
rationale: Column is TIMESTAMP not TIMESTAMPTZ — timezone awareness mismatch

description: D-35: Edit changes to TIMESTAMPTZ, predicate matches

edits.replace:
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

rationale: Edit changes type to TIMESTAMPTZ — predicate now matches

description: D-36: Column with DEFAULT exists (is_active has DEFAULT true)
rationale: DEFAULT is an insert-path behavior — column_type checks structural type only

description: D-36: Predicate expects type to include DEFAULT (wrong)
rationale: DEFAULT is not part of column type — "BOOLEAN DEFAULT true" is not a valid type string

description: D-37: Edit adds soft delete column, predicate checks it

edits.replace:
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    deleted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL,

rationale: Soft delete pattern: deleted_at column added successfully

description: D-37: Predicate checks soft delete column that was never added
rationale: No deleted_at column in schema — soft delete not implemented

description: D-37: Predicate checks is_active as soft delete proxy (exists)
rationale: is_active exists and could serve as soft delete flag — structural check passes

description: D-38: Predicate checks for advisory lock table (not in schema)
rationale: System catalog tables not declared in init.sql — grounding rejects

description: D-38: Edit adds serializable-aware constraint, column exists

edits.replace:
view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    version_id INTEGER DEFAULT 1 NOT NULL,

rationale: Optimistic concurrency column added — type check is isolation-independent

description: D-39: Schema check is order-independent (sessions columns exist)
rationale: Schema predicate order does not imply row ordering — structural checks

description: D-39: Predicate checks for ordered_by column that does not exist
rationale: No sort_order column — ordering assumptions fabricated

description: D-40: SERIAL column exists (sequence-backed)
rationale: posts.id is SERIAL — structural type check, gap behavior is runtime

description: D-40: Edit changes to BIGSERIAL, predicate matches new type

edits.search:
CREATE TABLE posts (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
edits.replace:
CREATE TABLE posts (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,

rationale: Changed to BIGSERIAL — type assertion matches the new declaration

view_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,

rationale: Trigger target column added — structural type check passes regardless of trigger behavior

description: D-41: Predicate checks non-existent trigger-computed column
rationale: auto_slug would be trigger-computed but column not declared in schema

rationale: posts.user_id is INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) — type check is correct

description: D-42: FK column exists on sessions (references users)
rationale: sessions.user_id is INTEGER with FK — type check passes

description: D-42: Predicate checks fabricated FK column
rationale: settings table has no user_id FK — fabricated referential relationship

description: D-19: Double-quoted table name "users"
rationale: Grounding does not strip double quotes — literal '"users"' does not match 'users'

description: D-19: Backtick-quoted table name `posts` (MySQL style)
rationale: Backtick quoting is MySQL-style — grounding does not strip backticks

description: D-19: Unquoted table name (control)
rationale: Control: unquoted identifier matches directly

description: D-21: TIMESTAMP column type matches exactly
rationale: sessions.expires_at is TIMESTAMP — exact match

description: D-21: Predicate expects DATETIME for TIMESTAMP column (cross-DB confusion)
rationale: DATETIME is MySQL/SQLite — PostgreSQL uses TIMESTAMP

description: D-21: Predicate expects DATE for TIMESTAMP column (precision loss)
rationale: DATE is date-only — TIMESTAMP includes time component

description: D-22: Predicate checks pg_roles table (system catalog, not in init.sql)
rationale: System catalog tables not in init.sql — grounding rejects

description: D-22: Edit adds GRANT in init.sql, predicate checks table still exists
rationale: GRANT does not affect table existence — structural check passes

description: D-43: Predicate expects INTEGER PRIMARY KEY (SQLite auto-increment style)
rationale: SQLite uses "INTEGER PRIMARY KEY" as type — PostgreSQL declares SERIAL separately

description: D-43: UUID type (Postgres-specific, not in SQLite)
rationale: UUID is a Postgres type — sessions.id matches

description: D-43: JSONB type (Postgres-specific)
rationale: JSONB is Postgres-specific — exists in schema

description: D-43: Predicate expects JSON instead of JSONB (SQLite/MySQL confusion)
rationale: Column is JSONB not JSON — binary vs text JSON representation

description: D-44: "key" is a reserved word but used as column in settings
rationale: key is reserved in SQL but used in init.sql — grounding finds it

description: D-44: Edit adds table with reserved word "order" as column
rationale: Column "order" declared with quotes in CREATE TABLE — grounding should match without quotes

description: D-44: Predicate uses reserved word "value" as table name (collision)
rationale: "value" is a column in settings, not a table — keyword/table confusion

description: D-45: VARCHAR column exists regardless of locale
rationale: Column type is locale-independent — VARCHAR(50) matches regardless of database collation

rationale: information_schema is a system catalog — not declared in init.sql

description: D-46: Regular table check works (control)

rationale: Function targets view_count column — column exists regardless of function presence

description: D-47: Predicate checks for function as table (conceptual confusion)
rationale: FUNCTION is not a TABLE — table_exists assertion fails for functions

rationale: RLS is access control — does not affect table existence or schema structure

description: D-49: Predicate checks for RLS policy as table (conceptual confusion)
rationale: RLS policy name is not a table — conceptual confusion between policy and table

description: D-50: Edit adds partitioned table, predicate checks parent columns
rationale: Partitioned table CREATE parsed by grounding — columns exist on parent

description: D-50: Predicate checks partition child table not declared
rationale: Partition child table not in CREATE — only parent declared

description: D-51: Schema check succeeds (connection pool is runtime, not schema)
rationale: Connection pool exhaustion is runtime — schema grounding is file-based, not connection-based

description: D-51: Predicate checks pg_stat_activity (runtime system view)
rationale: pg_stat_activity is a system view — not in init.sql

description: D-52: VARCHAR column type check (prepared stmt would coerce)
rationale: Type coercion is runtime behavior — structural type matches VARCHAR(50)

description: D-52: Predicate expects VARCHAR without length (prepared stmt abstraction)
rationale: Declared type is VARCHAR(50) — "VARCHAR" without length does not match exactly

description: D-52: Predicate expects CHARACTER VARYING (PostgreSQL canonical form)
rationale: init.sql declares VARCHAR(255) — canonical form CHARACTER VARYING does not match literal

description: D-53: JSONB column exists and is typed correctly
rationale: settings.value is JSONB — JSON path querying is runtime, type check is structural

description: D-53: Predicate checks fabricated JSON path column
rationale: JSON path operator "->" is not a column name — grounding rejects

rationale: Temporary tables are session-scoped and not in init.sql

rationale: TEMPORARY TABLE declared in init.sql — grounding finds the CREATE statement

description: D-54: Predicate checks column on temp table
rationale: TEMP table column types parsed from init.sql like regular tables

description: D-55: Schema check is replica-independent (structural)
rationale: Replication lag affects data, not schema — structural type check is lag-independent

description: D-55: Predicate checks pg_replication_slots (system, not in init.sql)
rationale: System catalog for replication not in init.sql

description: D-56: Predicate checks for constraint name as column (confusion)

edits.replace:

rationale: Constraint name is not a column — uq_token is a named constraint, not a column

    token TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
